page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-hypervised server features 4 dedicated vCPUs with 4 physical cores and a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes no local storage and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA-NI.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth scaling. Single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the average range, alongside single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core, is positioned in the lower tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also show weaker throughput. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput are relatively low.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c7t.xlarge provides a dedicated compute profile with a low RAM-to-vCPU ratio, making it a resource-dense option for workloads that do not require large memory footprints or local storage. Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, this instance is best suited for lightweight compute-intensive tasks, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and minor cryptographic or text processing workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 8.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and lacks local storage, relying on network-attached storage with a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. The processor supports AVX-512 VNNI and SHA-NI instructions. Benchmarks show average single-core performance in Geekbench and stress-ng, but weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated compute workloads with low memory requirements. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, and basic cryptographic processing.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring Intel Xeon processors, designed for single-threaded tasks and lightweight workloads requiring a low memory footprint.
